iso_3166.finding: ISO 3166-2 does NOT contain dual listings. Crimea is listed EXCLUSIVELY under Ukraine: UA-43 (Autonomous Republic of Crimea) and UA-40 (Sevastopol). Russia's ISO 3166-2:RU has 83 subdivisions — no RU-CR, no RU-SEV. The ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency has never added Russian codes for any occupied Ukrainian territory. CLDR (used by every browser and OS) confirms: 83 Russian subdivisions, zero Crimea. In 2014, ISO actually reinforced Ukrainian framing by renaming the entry from 'Respublika Krym' to 'Avtonomna Respublika Krym'.

ukraine_codes.UA-43: Avtonomna Respublika Krym (Autonomous Republic of Crimea) — active

iso_3166.note: Russia domestically claims 89 federal subjects (including Crimea + 4 oblasts). ISO recognizes only 83.
iso_3166.sap_reference: SAP KB Article 2518366 confirms no ISO codes for Russian-administered Crimea

key_legislation.title: Council Regulation (EU) No 692/2014 concerning restrictions on the import of goods originating in Crimea or Sevastopol
Council Decision 2014/386/CFSP concerning restrictions on goods originating in Crimea or Sevastopol
Council Decision 2014/145/CFSP concerning restrictive measures in respect of actions undermining or threatening the territorial integrity, sovereignty and independence of Ukraine
Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 — Digital Services Act
key_legislation.crimea_classification: Art 34: VLOPs must assess systemic risks including threats to civic discourse
key_legislation.relevance: Maps showing Crimea as Russia = potential systemic risk under Art 34

eurlex.finding: The EU has adopted 50+ legal acts since 2014 that explicitly classify Crimea as illegally annexed Ukrainian territory. Council Regulation 692/2014 prohibits import of goods 'originating in Crimea or Sevastopol.' Yet platforms operating under EU jurisdiction (Google, Apple, Mapbox) show Crimea as 'disputed' or 'Russian' depending on worldview settings.

ofac.description: Executive Order 13685 — Blocking Property of Certain Persons and Prohibiting Certain Transactions With Respect to the Crimea Region of Ukraine
ofac.note: OFAC explicitly calls it 'the Crimea Region of UKRAINE' in the program name
ofac.key_finding: The US sanctions program title itself says 'Crimea Region of Ukraine'

regulation_gap.legal_position: Unambiguous — Crimea is Ukrainian territory (UN GA 68/262, EU Reg 692/2014, OFAC EO13685, ECHR 2021)
regulation_gap.technical_standards: Correct — ISO 3166-2 lists Crimea only under Ukraine (UA-43). CLDR confirms. GENC follows US policy (Ukraine).
regulation_gap.library_systems: Correct — LoC uses 'Crimea (Ukraine)' as canonical form
regulation_gap.institution_registries: Mostly correct — ROR codes 13/14 Crimean institutions as UA
regulation_gap.geodata_supply_chain: INCORRECT — Natural Earth assigns SOVEREIGNT=Russia, overriding ISO 3166
regulation_gap.downstream_platforms: Inconsistent — inherit Natural Earth's classification (D3, Leaflet, Plotly = Russia; Nominatim = Ukraine)
regulation_gap.academic_metadata: INCORRECT — papers use 'Republic of Crimea, Russia' despite institution registries saying Ukraine
regulation_gap.gap: The gap is NOT in international standards (ISO is correct). The gap is in GEODATA bypassing standards: Natural Earth ignores ISO 3166 and assigns Crimea to Russia based on 'de facto control'. This single dataset then propagates to 30M+ npm downloads/week. No regulatory mechanism requires geodata projects to follow ISO or international law.
